rationale: Ground truth has no real concerns (just an lgtm approve), so both trivially satisfy recall. X correctly matches the actual outcome with rigorous, grounded verification (cross-checked upstream vLLM naming, confirmed no missed rename sites, validated existing test updates) and reaches APPROVE, mirroring the human reviewer. Y's single finding (missing chat-completion fallback test) is valid and highly concrete/actionable, but it escalates a self-labeled 'minor' gap to a blocking REQUEST_CHANGES verdict on a mechanical bugfix PR that was actually approved — a real miscalibration — and the output is defectively triplicated (identical review text repeated three times), undermining reliability.
